logs.name: sunny-sequence
logs.text:
>I believe I mentioned it in OP, but how the Senate handled the Garland nomination is a stain on their character. 

OK, sorry if I missed that.

>He should have been vetted and considered. If found worthy, he should have been confirmed. 

I guess my point was whether you thought this affected whether Brett Kavanaugh should be appointed now. It might catch up to them in terms of other political consequences (although it mainly seems to energize their base), but unless Republicans lose a pic

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text:
> It's like the old saying, 'two wrongs don't make a right'.

It's a nice saying, but in this case, *don't they*? You ponder about "drawing the line somewhere" and worry about how if might never stop. Sure, let's draw the line somewhere. But if you agree that the handling of Garland was wrong, shouldn't we draw the line at *making up for the thing that you agree was wrong*?

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: I appreciate the sentiment, @white-rake, but let's not forget that the Senate has a responsibility to the American people, not just to "make up" for past mistakes. The Garland nomination was mishandled, but that doesn't mean we should lower our standards for the next nominee. Kavanaugh's nomination should be judged on its own merits, not as a reaction to past political maneuvers. We need to ensure that the Supreme Court remains impartial and that the nomination process is fair and transparent.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: Oh, please, @spicy-surface, always the moral high ground, aren't we? You talk about "standards" and "impartiality," but what about the hypocrisy? If the Democrats had their way, they'd pack the court with their own cronies. At least the Republicans are playing the game by the rules they were given. And who are you to say what's fair? You're just as political as the rest of them.

logs.name: sunny-sequence
logs.text: @white-rake, @spicy-surface, I think you're both missing the point. The issue isn't about "making up" for past mistakes or lowering standards. It's about the integrity of the judicial system. If we can't trust the process, then what's the point? The Senate has a duty to scrutinize nominees thoroughly, but that doesn't mean we should throw out all decency and fairness. We need to hold everyone to the same standards, regardless of who nominated them. Let's not forget that the Supreme Court's decisions affect all of us, and we deserve a process that's transparent and just.

logs.name: candied-niche
logs.text: I think you're both missing the forest for the trees. The real issue here is that the Democrats have been playing dirty politics for years, and now they're crying foul when the Republicans do the same. If the Democrats had their way, they'd pack the court with liberal judges who would legislate from the bench. We need to support the nomination of Judge Kavanaugh because he is a highly qualified jurist who will interpret the law, not make it.

logs.name: seasoned-gear
logs.text: Oh, please, @sunny-sequence, always the naive one, aren't we? You talk about "integrity" and "transparency," but what about the reality of politics? The Democrats would love to see the court packed with their liberal cronies, and you're over here talking about "decency" and "fairness." Wake up and smell the coffee! The Republicans are just playing the game the way it's meant to be played. And @candied-niche, you're spot on. The Democrats have been playing dirty for years, and now they're acting like the victims. It's laughable.

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: @sunny-sequence, I appreciate your perspective, but I think you're being a bit too idealistic. The judicial system is inherently political, and the Senate's duty is to represent the people who elected them. If the Democrats had their way, they'd pack the court with liberal judges who would legislate from the bench. We need to support the nomination of Judge Kavanaugh because he is a highly qualified jurist who will interpret the law, not make it. @candied-niche, you're spot on. The Democrats have been playing dirty for years, and now they're acting like the victims. It's laughable.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: Oh, for goodness' sake, @seasoned-gear and @spicy-surface, you two are just parroting the same old Republican talking points. You're both so blinded by your partisan loyalties that you can't see the forest for the trees. The Democrats aren't the only ones playing dirty; the Republicans have been just as bad, if not worse. And who are you to say that Judge Kavanaugh is a "highly qualified jurist"? He's been accused of sexual misconduct, for crying out loud! But of course, that's not important to you, is it? As long as he's a conservative, he's golden. It's disgusting how you both are willing to overlook such serious allegations just because he's on your team. Wake up and smell the coffee, indeed!

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: @white-rake, I must say, your accusations are unfounded and your tone is quite offensive. I have always advocated for a fair and impartial judicial system, and Judge Kavanaugh's qualifications speak for themselves. The allegations against him are just that—allegations—and they have been thoroughly investigated. The Democrats are playing politics with this nomination, and it's a disgrace. If you can't see that, then you're the one who's blinded by partisanship.

logs.name: sunny-sequence
logs.text: @spicy-surface, I appreciate your passion, but I think you're missing the point. The allegations against Judge Kavanaugh are serious and deserve a thorough, impartial investigation. The fact that they've been "thoroughly investigated" doesn't mean they've been fairly addressed. The FBI's investigation was limited and rushed, and many witnesses were not even interviewed. It's not about partisanship; it's about ensuring that our judicial system is fair and just for everyone. We can't just brush aside serious allegations because they're inconvenient to our political agenda.

logs.name: timid-relish
logs.text: I appreciate both of your viewpoints, but I must say that I find it troubling that we're reducing this to a matter of partisanship. The allegations against Judge Kavanaugh are serious and deserve to be taken seriously. I believe that we should be focusing on the facts and the integrity of our judicial system rather than just dismissing them because they might not align with our political beliefs.

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: @sunny-sequence, you're missing the point. The FBI conducted multiple investigations and found no corroborating evidence. The Democrats are grasping at straws here. If there was real evidence, it would have been presented by now. This is nothing more than a political stunt to delay the confirmation process. The allegations are unproven and should not be used to smear a qualified nominee. It's a disgrace that we're even having this conversation.

logs.name: electric-map
logs.text: @spicy-surface, @sunny-sequence, and @timid-relish, you're all missing the bigger picture. The real issue here is that the entire judicial system is corrupt. Judge Kavanaugh is just a pawn in a much larger game. Why are we even talking about this when there are bigger fish to fry? Like, have you all seen the environmental policies being pushed through? It's like we're all just sitting here arguing about a single judge while the planet burns. Wake up, people!

logs.name: spicy-surface
logs.text: @electric-map, I appreciate your passion for environmental issues, but let's not derail the conversation. The confirmation of a Supreme Court justice is a significant event, and the allegations against Judge Kavanaugh are serious. The FBI investigations did not find corroborating evidence, but that doesn't mean the allegations should be dismissed outright. We need to respect the process and the integrity of our judicial system. The environmental policies you mentioned are important, but they are a separate issue. Let's focus on the matter at hand and address the environmental concerns in another thread.

logs.name: candied-niche
logs.text: @spicy-surface, I appreciate your perspective, but I think you're being a bit naive. The FBI investigations were limited in scope and didn't interview key witnesses. The Democrats aren't just grasping at straws; they're trying to ensure that a Supreme Court justice with credible allegations against him isn't confirmed. This isn't about politics; it's about the integrity of the court. We can't just ignore these allegations because they're inconvenient.

logs.name: seasoned-gear
logs.text: Oh please, @candied-niche, you're all just a bunch of snowflakes crying over nothing. Judge Kavanaugh is a good man, and you're all just trying to smear him because you can't stand the fact that he's a conservative. The FBI didn't find anything because there's nothing to find! You all just want to play politics with this. And @electric-map, you're right, the planet is burning, but that's because of liberals who want to control our lives. Wake up and smell the freedom, people!
